sourceMovie.title: Hitch
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hitch (2005) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Andy Tennant's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Breakfast at Tiffany's
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hitch clicked for you, Breakfast at Tiffany's (1961) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hitch?
faq.answer: Try Breakfast at Tiffany's, Annie Hall, Chocolat—each shares DNA with Hitch while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hitch?
faq.answer: Hitch (2005) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hitch worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
